verdict: The demo can run 100% on live, keyless, verified-today APIs: ISRIC SoilGrids v2.0 (point soil properties + WRB soil classification, CORS *), the Government of India Soil Health Card GraphQL endpoint at soilhealth4.dac.gov.in (district-wise N/P/K/OC/pH/micronutrient status, data updated 2026-07-07 — yesterday — genuine live govt data, keyless, CORS-friendly), and NASA POWER agroclimate (daily temp/rain/humidity/solar for any Indian lat/lon). Satellite sources (Bhuvan, Bhoonidhi, MNCFC) are login/token-gated and are production-roadmap/deck material only. The one trap: SoilGrids returns null on nodata pixels (both suggested test points did!) — the demo must retry with a ~0.05° offset or fall back to district defaults; use lon=77.10&lat=23.25 (Bhopal, Vertisols/black soil, pH 7.4) as the golden demo point.
demo_plan:
Build one Next.js server route, e.g. /api/soil-profile?lat=23.25&lon=77.10, that does three parallel server-side fetches (all keyless, all verified live today):
1) SoilGrids properties: GET https://rest.isric.org/soilgrids/v2.0/properties/query?lon={lon}&lat={lat}&property=phh2o&property=nitrogen&property=soc&property=clay&property=sand&property=cec&depth=0-5cm&depth=5-15cm&value=mean&value=uncertainty — parse properties.layers[].depths[].values.mean and DIVIDE by layers[].unit_measure.d_factor (phh2o/clay/sand/soc/cec: d_factor 10; nitrogen: 100). CRITICAL: values.mean can be null on nodata pixels (77.08,23.20 and Ludhiana 75.85,30.90 both returned null) — on null, retry at lat/lon +0.05, then fall back to embedded district defaults. Golden demo point: lon=77.10&lat=23.25 → pH 7.4, clay 38.7%, CEC 40.5 cmol/kg.
2) SoilGrids classification: GET https://rest.isric.org/soilgrids/v2.0/classification/query?lon={lon}&lat={lat}&number_classes=3 → wrb_class_name ("Vertisols" at demo point) + wrb_class_probability [["Vertisols",43],...]. Map WRB class → Indian soil type via the crosswalk in embed_data (Vertisols→black/regur etc.) — this powers the "your soil is black cotton soil" farmer-facing line.
3) NASA POWER: GET https://power.larc.nasa.gov/api/temporal/daily/point?parameters=T2M,T2M_MAX,T2M_MIN,PRECTOTCORR,RH2M,ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN&community=AG&longitude={lon}&latitude={lat}&start=20260601&end=20260707&format=JSON → properties.parameter.T2M["20260601"]=33.71 etc.; fill_value is -999 (filter it). For crop-suitability normals use /api/temporal/climatology/point with the same params.
4) Soil Health Card (the credibility centerpiece — live Indian govt data): POST https://soilhealth4.dac.gov.in/ with JSON body {"query":"query { getNutrientDashboardForPortal(state: \"63f8e729dbc0f7fe0670d050\", cycle: \"2025-26\") }"} → array of every district in MP with results.n/p/k {High,Low,Medium} sample counts, OC, pH {Alkaline,Acidic,Neutral}, EC {Saline,NonSaline}, S/Fe/Zn/Cu/B/Mn {Sufficient,Deficient}, district{_id,name,code}, updatedAt (2026-07-07). Get state ids from {"query":"query { getState }"}; cycles from getCycles (["2026-27"]; "2025-26" also works). Compute e.g. "72% of Bhopal samples are low in nitrogen, 63% zinc-deficient" and feed it to Gemini. Cache the response as a JSON seed file at build time so the demo survives if the endpoint hiccups, but fetch live first — "live from soilhealth.dac.gov.in, updated yesterday" is a killer demo line. CORS reflects any origin, so even client-side fetch works, but keep it server-side.
Merge all four into one soil-profile JSON and pass to Gemini with the embed_data soil-type/crop table as grounding for the crop recommendation.
production_plan: Production keeps SoilGrids + NASA POWER as the global-fallback layer (both are officially open, no key, production-grade with fair-use caching — cache SoilGrids per 250m cell, it's static data). The India-first upgrades: (1) Soil Health Card — the soilhealth4.dac.gov.in GraphQL endpoint is the same backend the official dashboard uses but is undocumented; production should formalize via a data-sharing request to DA&FW (Dept of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare) or pull the published SHC datasets on data.gov.in with a free registered API key (api.data.gov.in was unreachable from this session's network so unverified today; registration is instant and free). Keep the GraphQL path as the live feed with the seed snapshot as fallback since govt endpoints change without notice. (2) Satellite: register (free) at bhuvan-app1.nrsc.gov.in/api/ for a Bhuvan token — gives LULC, NDVI/vegetation WMS layers and village-level services; genuinely programmatic but token-gated, so production-only. Bhoonidhi (bhoonidhi.nrsc.gov.in) for raw ResourceSat/Sentinel scene downloads (login required, batch pipeline not runtime API). MNCFC/FASAL crop-forecast products are PDF/report-grade — deck citation only. (3) Zonation: the Planning Commission 15-zone scheme (embedded) for coarse logic; ICAR-NARP's 127 agro-climatic sub-zones (via ICAR/KVK datasets) for district-precision recommendations in production.

findings.verification_note: Fetched live. GeoJSON Feature; properties.layers[] each has name, unit_measure{d_factor,mapped_units,target_units}, depths[]{label:'0-5cm', values:{mean,uncertainty}}. At 77.10,23.25 got phh2o mean=74 (÷d_factor 10 = pH 7.4), clay=387 (38.7%), cec=405 (40.5 cmol/kg). CORS header access-control-allow-origin: * confirmed via preflight with Origin kisanvaani.vercel.app. Keyless.
findings.notes: TRAP: both obvious test points (77.08,23.20 and Ludhiana 75.85,30.90) returned values.mean=null (nodata pixels). Demo MUST null-check and retry with ~0.05 deg offset or fall back to district defaults. Repeat property= and depth= params (not comma lists). Divide mean by d_factor (10 for most; 100 for nitrogen). Fair-use rate limit — cache per point.

findings.source_name: ISRIC SoilGrids v2.0 — WRB soil classification
findings.verification_note: Fetched live: {wrb_class_name:'Vertisols', wrb_class_value:29, wrb_class_probability:[['Vertisols',43],['Cambisols',17],['Fluvisols',8]]}. Vertisols = black cotton (regur) soil — correct for Bhopal region.
findings.notes: Map wrb_class_name to Indian soil type via crosswalk in embed_data to get the farmer-facing 'black soil / kali mitti' line and the crop shortlist.

findings.source_name: Soil Health Card GraphQL (DA&FW, Govt of India)
findings.url: POST https://soilhealth4.dac.gov.in/ (also /graphql) — queries: getState, getCycles, getNutrientDashboardForPortal(state:"<ObjectId>", cycle:"2025-26")
findings.verification_note: Fetched live. getState returned 34 states with _id/name/code (MP=63f8e729dbc0f7fe0670d050). getCycles returned ['2026-27']. getNutrientDashboardForPortal(state:MP, cycle:'2025-26') returned every MP district with results.{n,p,k:{High,Low,Medium}, OC:{High,Low,Medium}, pH:{Alkaline,Acidic,Neutral}, EC:{Saline,NonSaline}, S,Fe,Zn,Cu,B,Mn:{Sufficient,Deficient}}, district{_id,name,code}, updatedAt='2026-07-07T17:27:08Z' — updated YESTERDAY. CORS preflight reflects arbitrary origin (access-control-allow-origin echoed kisanvaani.vercel.app). Keyless.
findings.notes: This is the actual backend of the official SHC public dashboard — real live Indian govt nutrient data, the credibility centerpiece. Undocumented/unofficial, so cache a seed snapshot at build time as fallback. Values are sample COUNTS per class — compute percentages (e.g. % of district samples Low in N). Unknown query names return masked error 'Unable to connect to the server'; passing state NAME instead of ObjectId returns a mongodb-objectid validation error.

findings.verification_note: Fetched live. properties.parameter.T2M = {'20260601':33.71,...}, T2M_MAX up to 44.0C, PRECTOTCORR mm/day, RH2M %, ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN MJ/m2/day; header.fill_value=-999; parameters block gives units/longname. API v2.9.4. Keyless.
findings.notes: Use /api/temporal/climatology/point (same params minus start/end) for long-term monthly normals — better input for crop suitability than one recent fortnight. Filter -999 fill values.

findings.verification_note: HTTP 200 on fetch. Public dashboard site; no documented public REST API — the GraphQL endpoint above is what powers it.
findings.notes: Cite the scheme (Soil Health Card, DA&FW) in the deck; the GraphQL finding is the programmatic path.

findings.source_name: Agro-climatic zones of India (15-zone Planning Commission scheme)
findings.url: https://icar.org.in/ (ICAR, HTTP 200) / https://agriwelfare.gov.in/ (HTTP 200) — zone list embedded from standard classification, no single API
findings.verification_note: Did not fetch a page containing the literal 15-zone list; ICAR and DA&FW sites confirmed reachable for citation. The 15-zone list (embedded in embed_data) is the stable 1989 Planning Commission classification reproduced in all govt/ICAR literature.
findings.notes: Attribute to 'Planning Commission of India (1989)' not ICAR — ICAR-NARP is the separate 127-sub-zone scheme; saying 'ICAR 15 zones' in the deck would be a judgable citation error.
